blocks.content: The night before the funeral, he sat alone in the living room for a long time.
Everyone had gone. The house was quiet. Harold was on his shelf in the next room.
He opened it to a blank page.
He sat with the pencil in his hand for a very long time.

The part of him that drew was the same part that had held her — the part that noticed everything, cataloged everything, kept the account. That part of him was not available right now. It was fully occupied.
He looked at the room. The lamp she had loved that he had once found ugly and then been wrong about. The mismatched mugs on the shelf. The keys by the door.
The residue of a life shared completely. A life that had been exactly what she asked for.
Both things were entirely true and neither one canceled the other.
